project.name: onegraph-ast-pipeline
project.description: Stage 1 of the OneGraph compiler pipeline — AST-based architectural fact extractor for JS/JSX repositories

nodes.name: CallExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines an AST extractor that normalizes function calls, constructor invocations, and JSX component usages into call facts.

nodes.name: ClassExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines an AST extractor that captures class declarations and expressions, including superclass and method metadata.

nodes.name: ExportExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines an AST extractor that collects exported names and default or re-export metadata from export statements.

nodes.name: FunctionExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines an AST extractor that resolves named functions, methods, and React-style components across several JavaScript function forms.

nodes.name: IExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract extractor contract used by the walker for node-type registration, fact extraction, and fact storage keys.

nodes.name: ImportExtractor.js
nodes.summary: Defines an AST extractor that parses ES module import declarations into normalized source and specifier facts.

nodes.name: CallExtractor
nodes.summary: Concrete extractor for call expressions, constructor calls, and JSX component references.

nodes.name: ClassExtractor
nodes.summary: Concrete extractor for class declarations and class expressions with inheritance and method summaries.

nodes.name: ExportExtractor
nodes.summary: Concrete extractor for export statements and public API symbol discovery.

nodes.name: FunctionExtractor
nodes.summary: Concrete extractor for declarations, expressions, arrow functions, and class methods.

nodes.name: IExtractor
nodes.summary: Abstract base contract that all fact extractors extend and implement.

nodes.name: ImportExtractor
nodes.summary: Concrete extractor for ES module import declarations and imported binding specifiers.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Returns the Tree-sitter node types that the call extractor handles.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Returns the fact bucket key used to store extracted call facts.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Dispatches each supported AST node to the appropriate call, constructor, or JSX extraction helper.

nodes.name: _fromCallExpression
nodes.summary: Converts a call expression into a normalized function, method, or expression call fact.

nodes.name: _fromNewExpression
nodes.summary: Converts a constructor invocation into a normalized constructor call fact.

nodes.name: _fromJsxElement
nodes.summary: Extracts a JSX component usage from a standard opening and closing JSX element.

nodes.name: _fromJsxSelfClosing
nodes.summary: Extracts a JSX component usage from a self-closing JSX element.

nodes.name: _jsxFact
nodes.summary: Builds a shared JSX component call fact and filters out lowercase intrinsic tags.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Returns the Tree-sitter node types that the class extractor handles.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Returns the fact bucket key used to store extracted class facts.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Resolves class names, superclass references, and method metadata from class AST nodes.

nodes.name: resolveVariableName
nodes.summary: Resolves the variable binding name for an anonymous class expression assigned to a declarator.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Returns the Tree-sitter node types that the export extractor handles.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Returns the fact bucket key used to store extracted export facts.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Collects exported symbol names, default-export state, and re-export source metadata.

nodes.name: stripQuotes
nodes.summary: Removes surrounding quote characters from a module source literal.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Returns the Tree-sitter node types that the function extractor handles.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Returns the fact bucket key used to store extracted function facts.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Dispatches supported function and method nodes to the appropriate extraction strategy.

nodes.name: _fromDeclaration
nodes.summary: Builds a fact for a named function or generator declaration and marks likely React components.

nodes.name: _fromParentVariable
nodes.summary: Builds a fact for a function expression or arrow function using its parent variable binding.

nodes.name: _fromMethodDef
nodes.summary: Builds a fact for a class method and resolves its enclosing class name.

nodes.name: resolveVariableName
nodes.summary: Finds the nearest variable declarator name for an assigned function expression or arrow function.

nodes.name: resolveParentClassName
nodes.summary: Walks upward through parent nodes to resolve the enclosing class for a method definition.

nodes.name: isComponent
nodes.summary: Applies the uppercase-name heuristic used to classify likely React components.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Abstract contract method for declaring which AST node types an extractor wants to receive.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Abstract contract method for converting a matching syntax node into a plain fact object.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Abstract contract method for naming the fact collection that stores an extractor's output.

nodes.name: getNodeTypes
nodes.summary: Returns the Tree-sitter node types that the import extractor handles.

nodes.name: getFactKey
nodes.summary: Returns the fact bucket key used to store extracted import facts.

nodes.name: extract
nodes.summary: Collects the imported module source and all declared import specifiers from an import statement.

nodes.name: _collectSpecifiers
nodes.summary: Normalizes default, named, and namespace import bindings from an import clause.

nodes.name: stripQuotes
nodes.summary: Removes surrounding quote characters from an import source literal.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Defines ignored working artifacts so dependencies, generated output, and log files are excluded from repository tracking.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that wires concrete parser, walker, extractor, discovery, and writer implementations into the analysis pipeline and runs it against a source directory.

nodes.name: fatal error handler
nodes.summary: Promise rejection handler for pipeline execution that logs a fatal pipeline error and exits the CLI with a non-zero status.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Declares the OneGraph AST pipeline package metadata, its CLI entry point, Node.js engine requirement, analysis script, and tree-sitter dependencies.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Project-specific ignore template for understand-anything analysis, documenting optional exclusion patterns for generated outputs and test files.

nodes.name: IFactWriter.js
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract fact-writer contract that decouples pipeline execution from the concrete persistence mechanism.

nodes.name: IFactWriter
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for persisting extracted file facts to a destination chosen by a concrete writer.

nodes.name: write
nodes.summary: Abstract persistence method that concrete writers must implement to store collected facts.

nodes.name: JsonFactWriter.js
nodes.summary: Implements IFactWriter by serializing all extracted facts as a pretty-printed JSON array on disk.

nodes.name: JsonFactWriter
nodes.summary: Concrete fact writer that creates the destination directory and writes facts using configurable JSON indentation.

nodes.name: write
nodes.summary: Ensures the output directory exists and writes the facts array to a UTF-8 JSON file.

nodes.name: micro-lap-facts.json
nodes.summary: Large sample facts export showing the pipeline's per-file JSON schema for imports, exports, functions, calls, and classes.

nodes.name: IParser.js
nodes.summary: Defines the parser abstraction used by the pipeline to turn source files into language-agnostic parse results.

nodes.name: IParser
nodes.summary: Abstract parser contract for reading a file and returning a normalized parse result plus supported extensions.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Abstract parse operation that converts one source file into a parse result.

nodes.name: getSupportedExtensions
nodes.summary: Abstract capability query used by the registry to map file extensions to parser implementations.

nodes.name: ParserRegistry.js
nodes.summary: Maintains the extension-to-parser lookup used by the pipeline to select a parser for each discovered source file.

nodes.name: ParserRegistry
nodes.summary: Registry that stores parser instances by file extension and exposes lookup and introspection helpers.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers a parser for every extension it advertises, allowing later overrides.

nodes.name: getParserFor
nodes.summary: Selects the parser instance associated with a file path's extension.

nodes.name: registeredExtensions
nodes.summary: Returns the set of extensions currently handled by the registry.

nodes.name: TreeSitterParser.js
nodes.summary: Concrete JavaScript/JSX parser that uses tree-sitter to produce parse results for downstream traversal.

nodes.name: TreeSitterParser
nodes.summary: IParser implementation backed by tree-sitter-javascript for .js and .jsx files.

nodes.name: getSupportedExtensions
nodes.summary: Advertises JavaScript and JSX extensions handled by this parser.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Reads source text from disk and parses it into a tree-sitter syntax tree packaged as a parse result.

nodes.name: AnalysisPipeline.js
nodes.summary: Coordinates discovery, parser selection, AST walking, and fact writing for an end-to-end analysis run.

nodes.name: AnalysisPipeline
nodes.summary: Dependency-injected orchestrator that processes discovered files, accumulates facts, and persists the final result.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Executes the full pipeline, tracking parsed, skipped, and errored files before writing the collected facts.

nodes.name: FileDiscovery.js
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a source tree and collects files whose extensions match the configured language set.

nodes.name: FileDiscovery
nodes.summary: Filesystem traversal utility that skips hidden or noisy directories while gathering matching source files.

nodes.name: discover
nodes.summary: Public entry point that accumulates matching files under a root directory.

nodes.name: _walk
nodes.summary: Recursive directory walker that filters unreadable, hidden, and excluded paths before collecting matches.

nodes.name: IASTWalker.js
nodes.summary: Defines the AST traversal abstraction that turns parse results into structured file facts.

nodes.name: IASTWalker
nodes.summary: Abstract traversal contract for walkers that visit syntax trees and emit file-level facts.

nodes.name: walk
nodes.summary: Abstract traversal method that accepts a parse result and returns extracted facts.

nodes.name: TreeSitterWalker.js
nodes.summary: Implements depth-first tree-sitter traversal with a dispatch table that routes nodes to interested extractors.

nodes.name: TreeSitterWalker
nodes.summary: Concrete AST walker that builds an extractor dispatch map and accumulates facts during recursive traversal.

nodes.name: walk
nodes.summary: Initializes the file-facts container and starts traversal from the syntax tree root.

nodes.name: _visit
nodes.summary: Visits one node, dispatches to registered extractors, and recursively descends through all children.

layers.name: Entry & Orchestration
layers.description: CLI entry point and pipeline coordinator that wire all components together for end-to-end analysis runs.

layers.name: Parsing
layers.description: Parser abstractions, parser selection, and tree-sitter-backed source parsing for JavaScript and JSX files.

layers.name: AST Walking
layers.description: AST traversal contracts and tree-sitter walking logic that dispatch parsed nodes to extractors.

layers.name: Fact Extraction
layers.description: Extractor contracts and concrete AST extractors that derive imports, exports, functions, calls, and classes as facts.

layers.name: Output
layers.description: Fact writer abstractions, JSON serialization, and representative generated fact output artifacts.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project and analysis configuration files that define package metadata and ignore rules for repository and tooling behavior.

tour.title: Start with package metadata and analysis boundaries
tour.description: Begin in package.json to see that this stage is a Node 18 CLI built around tree-sitter and exposed through index.js. Then glance at .gitignore and .understandignore to understand which generated artifacts or noisy paths are intentionally kept out of version control and analysis.

tour.title: Read the composition root in index.js
tour.description: index.js is the dependency-injection root and the best mental starting point for the runtime architecture. It wires together discovery, parser registration, AST walking, extractors, and JSON output, then turns CLI arguments into a single pipeline run.

tour.title: Follow end-to-end orchestration and file discovery
tour.description: AnalysisPipeline.js shows the control flow for the whole stage: discover files, choose a parser, walk the parsed tree, and write accumulated facts. FileDiscovery.js explains the first filtering boundary by recursively finding only supported source files while skipping hidden folders and common build output.

tour.title: Understand how source files become parse results
tour.description: The parsing layer separates interface from implementation so the pipeline never hard-codes language details. Read IParser.js for the contract, ParserRegistry.js for extension-based parser selection, and TreeSitterParser.js for the concrete JavaScript/JSX parser that returns tree-sitter parse results.

tour.title: See how parsed trees are traversed and dispatched
tour.description: The walker layer turns one parse result into one file-level facts object. IASTWalker.js defines that contract, while TreeSitterWalker.js implements a depth-first traversal with a node-type dispatch table so only relevant extractors run for each syntax node.

tour.title: Study the extractor model and fact categories
tour.description: IExtractor.js defines the extension point that keeps each fact category single-purpose and pluggable. The concrete extractors then map specific tree-sitter node types into normalized architectural facts for imports, exports, functions, calls and JSX usage, and classes.

tour.title: Finish at the output boundary and sample artifact
tour.description: The output layer shows how extracted facts leave the pipeline without coupling orchestration to one storage format. IFactWriter.js defines the persistence contract, JsonFactWriter.js implements JSON serialization, and micro-lap-facts.json gives you a concrete example of the final per-file schema emitted by this stage.
